chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No. 165 of 2022 Applicants: Shaikh Abdul Wajid s/o Shaikh Abdul Khaliq and Muhammad Ali Shaikh s/o Shaikh Abdul Wajid, through Mr. Khawaja Naveed Ahmed, advocate Respondent: The State, through Ms. Rahat Ehsan, Addl. Prosecutor General along with DSP Amjad Kalyar, Clifton Division, Karachi Complainant: Allah Dino s/o. Muhammad Bachal, through Mr. Jamal Muhammad advocate --------------- Date of hearing: 25.05.2022 Date of order: 25.05.2022 --------------- O R D E R ZAFAR AHMED RAJPUT, J:- Through instant Cr. Bail Application, applicants/ accused Shaikh Abdul Wajid s/o. Shaikh Abdul Khaliq and Muhammad Ali Shaikh s/o. Shaikh Abdul Wajid seek pre-arrest bail in Crime No. 536 of 2021 registered under Section 302, 34, P.P.C. at P.S. Gizri, Karachi. Their earlier bail application for the same relief bearing No. 4559 of 2021 was dismissed by the learned Additional Sessions Judge-X, Karachi-South vide order, dated 24.01.2022. They were admitted to interim pre-arrest bail by this Court vide order, dated 25.01.2022, now the matter is fixed for confirmation of interim bail or otherwise. 2. It is alleged that on 08.12.2021, the dead body of the Hafiz Shahid, the nephew of the complainant and servant of the applicants, was found in a servant quarter of bungalow No. 19/B, Street No.3, Khayaban-e-Bukhari, Phase-VI, DHA, Karachi, which is located in front of the applicant’s bungalow, for that instant F.I.R. was lodged. 3. It is an admitted position that after investigation, the I.O. has submitted his report for disposal of the case under “A” Class of Police Rules, and considering such fact the learned counsel for the complainant, at the very outset,

It is alleged that on 08.12.2021, the dead body of the Hafiz Shahid, the nephew of the complainant and servant of the applicants, was found in a servant quarter of bungalow No. 19/B, Street No.3, Khayaban-e-Bukhari, Phase-VI, DHA, Karachi, which is located in front of the applicant’s bungalow, for that instant F.I.R. was lodged. 3. It is an admitted position that after investigation, the I.O. has submitted his report for disposal of the case under “A” Class of Police Rules, and considering such fact the learned counsel for the complainant, at the very outset, 2 records his no objection for the confirmation of the interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants. 3. Accordingly, the interim bail already granted to the applicants vide order, dated 25.01.2022, is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. Cr. Bail Application stands disposed of. JUDGE Athar Zai
